economic_notes: Five government business entities accounted for by modified equity method: Takla Development Corporation (logging/silviculture), Sasuchan Development Corporation, Takla Lake Limited Partnership (units transferred to Takla Master Limited Partnership during year), Takla Master Limited Partnership, Tse Keh Nay LLP. Nation received BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing of $402,244.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Five GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. During the year, partnership units held in Takla Lake Limited Partnership were transferred to Takla Master Limited Partnership. Prior year (2021) included $4,042,556 income from GBEs (Takla Lake LP profit share), making prior year surplus unusually high. Term deposits of $16,000,000 purchased during the year. Dividends payable to minors of $728,000 secured by $1,000,000 term deposit. No schedule of remuneration included in the document. Annual debt service calculated as repayment of long-term debt $195,911 plus capital lease payments $32,809 = $228,720. Capital expenses of $1,225,239 reported as separate segment. total_government_transfers includes ISC $7,122,555 + FNHA $878,555 + CMHC $3,930,758 + Province of BC $123,705 + Carrier Sekani Family Services $1,088,082 + Carrier Sekani Tribal Council $3,063,712 + FNESC $623,418 + Other government within Other revenue (Public Safety Canada $626,969 + PGNAETA $192,605 + Union of BC Municipalities $170,570 + NDIT $113,107 + Environment and Climate Change Canada $207,021) = $17,830,745 approximately. OSR set to own source revenue line only.
